etymology.description: 声(声) (shēng, 'sound of stone chime') + 殳(殳) (shū, 'hand holding a hammer or a mallet') -> a stone chime being struck by a mallet
etymology.interpretation: The character 殸(qīng, 'sound of metal or stone') visually represents the process of producing sound by striking a stone chime with a mallet, illustrating the concept of sound in both form and meaning.

parts.english: hand holding a hammer or a mallet
